cot_prefix: Okay, let me try to work through this question. The question is asking what the dance project aims to do. The options are A to D. First, I need to recall the details from the passage. The passage talks about LaMar Baylor teaching dance to street children in Rwanda through the Rebecca Davis Dance Company. The project helps them learn more about dance and how to behave in a classroom. Rebecca Davis started this after visiting Rwanda and seeing street kids dancing. She thought dance could get them off the streets into a safe place. She also believes learning to dance is a step toward education. There's mention of scholarships for top students to attend Sunrise Boarding School, but about 30 have received financial aid. Also, other dance programs in Bosnia and Guinea are mentioned. So the options are: A gives parental care, B provides scholarships, C helps them receive education, D keeps them healthy. Looking at the passage, the main focus is on using dance as a way to help these kids. The key points are that Rebecca Davis believes dance is a step toward education. The children learn classroom behavior, which is part of getting them into an educational environment. Even though scholarships are mentioned (option B), those are for those who do best, but the overarching goal is education. The passage says the dance classes provide structured learning and self-expression, which they haven't had before. Also, after learning dance, they can take IT classes. So the main aim is to help them receive education. Option C seems
